title: Use Query-Ranking Only As An OOF Meta-Feature
summary: Race strategy is relative: a driver often pits because they are becoming the most vulnerable car in a local race context.
rationale: Race strategy is relative: a driver often pits because they are becoming the most vulnerable car in a local race context. Ranking objectives are designed for within-group ordering, but prior research already explored rankers as standalone specialists. A less brittle variant is to train a race-lap grouped ranker and feed its OOF score into the stronger classifier as one more feature.
implementation_hint: Create query groups at `(Race, Year, LapNumber)` or `(Race, Year, Lap bucket)` and train CatBoost ranking with group IDs and target `PitNextLap`. Generate strict OOF rank scores or percentile ranks, then add them to the main classifier together with simple peer-relative features. Evaluate whether the meta-feature helps only in dry races or only when many cars share the same compound.
expected_effect: Should capture local 'who is most pit-ready right now?' information without forcing the whole pipeline into a pure ranking objective.
risk: If group size is small or many laps have zero positives, the ranker may add noise; use it as an auxiliary feature, not the final predictor.
